name: Daily Seinfeld Sequence — Ongoing Entertainment Broadcast Engine

sections: DotCom Secrets Secret #8 — Daily Seinfeld Sequence
DotCom Secrets Secret #6 — The Attractive Character (identities, flaws, polarity, 6 storylines)
Funnel Hacker's Cookbook #24 — Follow-Up Funnel (Soap Opera + Daily Seinfeld)
Copywriting Secrets — Stealth/Columbo close, subject-line families, Ten Reasons People Buy

trigger.primary: Subscriber has COMPLETED the 5-email Soap Opera Sequence welcome autoresponder (book: 'After someone has completed your SOAP series, they should be moved to a broadcast list'). Implemented as the Soap Opera workflow firing its final step / applying a 'soap-opera-complete' tag, which GRADUATES the contact into the ongoing broadcast segment.
trigger.secondary: Any net-new buyer or already-engaged lead manually added to the 'seinfeld-broadcast' segment.
trigger.trigger_type_ghl: Tag Added: soap-opera-complete  (or Workflow-finished trigger from the Soap Opera workflow)

channels: Blog (repurpose the same email body verbatim — book: 'copy and paste their daily Seinfeld email')
SMS (optional teaser linking to the email/blog)
cadence: ONE broadcast per day, perpetual. Each send is a one-off tied to what is happening in the Attractive Character's life THAT day — NOT a pre-lined sequence everyone marches through. Ramp confidence monthly -> biweekly -> weekly -> daily (book: 'if I don't email my list every day, I lose money every day').
ratio: 90% entertainment / 10% content (book: after the Soap Opera bond, readers respond to 'nothing', not content-packed emails).

sequence.purpose: Earn the open with curiosity. 8 of 10 people only read the subject (Edwards/Ogilvy).
sequence.subjectAngle: Curiosity / open-loop. Real book examples: '[True Story] He FLUSHED $20 million down the toilet today' and 'Jiu Jitsu is like wrestling for old, fat guys (and other marketing stuff)'.
sequence.bodyFramework: Drop the reader into the middle of an entertaining moment at the point of high drama or absurdity.

sequence.purpose: Bond + entertain the engaged list daily so they stay happy to hear from you every day.
sequence.bodyFramework: A random, entertaining episode from the AC's day/life. Prompt menu from the book: something embarrassing; surviving the holidays; vacation planning; a purchase you regret; a purchase you adore; what made you rage yesterday that you laugh about today; crazy kid/dog antics; a funny thing from your past that teaches a lesson. Show character FLAWS and POLARITY. Format: one-two sentences per line, lots of white space, no long paragraphs, loads of personality, do not fear grammar slips (flaws = relatable).

sequence.purpose: Make money. EVERY story must tie back to something you sell (core offer, other product, or affiliate) — entertainment without a tie-in earns nothing.
sequence.bodyFramework: Stealth/Columbo pivot — 'So, why do I tell you this?...' — bridge the story's lesson to the offer using 'which means...' + at least one of the Ten Reasons People Buy. One single CTA link. Close with a P.S. that reinforces personality / adds an honest reason to act.

copyPersona.primary: Russell Brunson 'Attractive Character' / Seinfeld voice (DotCom Secrets Secrets #6-8). Pick one identity: Leader, Adventurer/Crusader, Reporter/Evangelist, or Reluctant Hero (Brunson's own = Reluctant Hero). 90/10 entertainment-to-content.
copyPersona.secondary: Jim Edwards (Copywriting Secrets) — supplies the Stealth/Columbo soft close, curiosity subject-line families (True Story / Warning / unexpected-comparison), pronoun audit (you/your not I/me), and the 'which means...' + Ten Reasons People Buy discipline so every tie-in is desire-driven.
scripts: 6 Attractive Character storylines (Loss & Redemption, Us vs Them, Before & After, Amazing Discovery, Secret Telling, Third-Person Testimonial)

ghlBuild.criticalNuance: FAITHFUL TO THE BOOK: Seinfeld emails are BROADCASTS, NOT autoresponders. 'Seinfeld emails are typically not lined up in a sequence that everyone has to go through.' In GoHighLevel this means daily one-off Email Campaigns sent to a Smart List — NOT a Workflow drip. The ONLY automation Skill 44 builds here is the graduation/segment-membership workflow.

graduationWorkflow.trigger: Tag Added 'soap-opera-complete' (or Workflow-finished from Soap Opera workflow)
graduationWorkflow.notes: No email steps in this workflow. Its only job is to move the contact into the broadcast segment. Set action order + parentKey before the first save (Skill 44 reliability).

ghlBuild.broadcastSegment: Smart List filtered: Tag = seinfeld-broadcast AND unsubscribed = false AND (NOT tag 'sunset'). This is the daily send audience.
ghlBuild.dailySend: Marketing > Emails > Email Campaign (Broadcast), composed that day (or pre-scheduled), sent to the Smart List. Skill 44 builds the reusable email template shell ('Seinfeld Broadcast Shell', see template seinfeld-broadcast-email) and the recurring compose-and-send SOP; broadcasts can be batch-scheduled.
ghlBuild.blogRepurpose: Optional automation (GHL workflow webhook or Zapier) pushing the same email body to the blog/CMS.
ghlBuild.engagementTracking: GHL auto-logs Email Opened / Link Clicked. Feed last-open into custom field last_email_open for the re-engagement / win-back template.

kpis: unsubscribe rate (acceptable churn signals over-mailing or off-persona)
faithfulness: Cadence (daily), 90/10 entertainment ratio, Hook-Story-Offer, broadcast-not-autoresponder, 'move to a broadcast list', blog repurpose, scannable formatting rules, the story-prompt menu, and BOTH example subject lines are taken directly from DotCom Secrets Secret #8. The Attractive Character identities/flaws/polarity/6 storylines are from Secret #6. GoHighLevel mechanics are the standard Skill-44 mapping of those concepts.
